cards.question: What investigations represent primary hemostasis?
cards.answer: Tourniquet test, bleeding time, platelet (platelet counts, platelet aggregation study, PFA-100/PFA-200), vWF(vWF antigen, vWF activity, Factor VIII)

cards.question: What happens in primary hemostasis?
cards.answer:
1.Exposure of subendothelial vWF and collagen
2.platelet adhesion (GPIb/GPIX/GPV-vWF, GPVI-collagen)
3.platelet activation & shape change (GPIIb/IIIa - fibrinogen/vWF) -> release cytokines for platelet activation (e.g. TXA2, ADP, serotonin) & aggregation (e.g. P2Y1, P2Y12) & inflammation
4.platelet aggregation
